verbatim_error: {"message":"Server Error: Sorry, this diff is temporarily unavailable due to heavy server load.","errors":[{"resource":"Comparison","field":"diff","code":"not_available"}],"documentation_url":"https://docs.github.com/rest/commits/commits#compare-two-commits","status":"500"}

sources.title: GitHub Compare Page Fails to Load When Creating PR – Stuck on Unicorn Screen (community Discussion #169082)
sources.relevance: Confirms GitHub's compare/diff RENDERING can fail (500/unicorn) on large or heavy diffs while the rest of the PR flow works. Recommended workaround: use the CLI or open the PR manually from the base branch — i.e. PR creation does not require the diff to render.

sources.title: 500 - Server Error: Sorry, this diff is taking too long to generate (github-changelog-generator #920)
sources.relevance: Independent report of the same diff-generation 500 from the compare endpoint; shows it is a transient server-side rendering failure, not a client/branch problem.

sources.title: GitHub availability report: April 2026 (GitHub Blog)
sources.relevance: GitHub publicly documents periodic 5xx incidents under load; transient 5xx from api.github.com is expected and clients are advised to retry.

sources.title: Compare two commits — GitHub REST API docs
sources.relevance: The documentation_url returned inside the 500 body. The compare endpoint renders a diff; under load it can return 500 with code "not_available".

sources.title: GitHub Status
sources.relevance: Official status page; brief api.github.com 5xx incidents are routine. Standard guidance is wait + retry.

conclusion: The HTTP 500 "this diff is temporarily unavailable due to heavy server load" / code "not_available" is a transient diff-RENDERING failure of the compare endpoint. It does NOT indicate the commits are missing or unindexed: the branch + commit were already pushed (the failure log shows `[origin/main: ahead 1]` and a successful `git push`), and `gh pr create` does not render the full diff, so it would have succeeded. The readiness gate must not treat this as fatal.
